derivation_notes: U-shape with serifs = cow face. Phoneme 'aa' from cow sound 'maa'. Tholkaappiyam confirms 'aa' as ancient morpheme for cow.
Tally of 4 marks = nanku (Tamil for four). First syllable 'na' used as phoneme. Enables encoding of 'nay/ney' (ghee).
Y-shaped sign. Appears consistently in dairy product inscriptions with unicorn motif. Seal 1076: thava thava ney = Very Good Ghee.
Cross/X sign. Becomes 'si' with e-vowel modifier (P060/M219), then 'Sivam/Sivan' with further modifier (M220).
Sign 216 (sa) + e-vowel modifier = sign 219 (si). Modifier has no sound of its own.
Double modifier on sign 216. Terminal compound in sa→si→sivam/sivan chain.
First stage of compound evolution 10→12→15. Sign 3246 documents full progression.
Intermediate compound sign. Evolved from sign 10 (kaa). Precursor to sign 15 (aakaavva).
Terminal integrated compound of 10+12. Seal 3246: vaya muu La aagaavva = carer of aged cows.
